title: Clockwork
description: Brasston village, the Grand Schedule, Sprocket the guide, Clem the Lamplighter, Madame Orrery's revelation.

lines: Brasston was a market town that ran like a pocket watch. The citizens moved on paths so ingrained they might as well have been grooves cut into the cobblestone.
Every citizen who passed them nodded at exactly the same moment in their walk, as if greeting strangers was a scheduled behavior.

lines.speaker: Sprocket
lines.text: Newcomers! Welcome to Brasston! You're — seven seconds late. My fault, I should have accounted for dimensional transition lag.

lines.speaker: Sprocket
lines.text: Everything here runs on the Grand Schedule. The Regulator set it up years ago. Or maybe always. Nobody remembers a time before the schedule.

lines.speaker: Sprocket
lines.text: Chief Engineer of the Grand Mechanism. Nobody sees them much, but you can feel them. In the way the gears turn. In the way the trains arrive.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Everything orderly and controlled and exactly where it's supposed to be?

lines.speaker: Sprocket
lines.text: You've met someone like the Regulator before?

lines.speaker: Madame Orrery
lines.text: Sit. We don't have much time. You never do, in this phase.

lines.speaker: Madame Orrery
lines.text: You've done this before. Different world, same shape. Same arrangement of power at the top. Same speech about order and purpose.

lines.speaker: Madame Orrery
lines.text: Whoever built these places — all of them — left the same fingerprints. The same design language. The same signature.

lines.speaker: Madame Orrery
lines.text: You keep winning and moving forward. And every time you win, the next world is harder. Doesn't that seem like something someone designed?
